---
title: "OpenClaw"
description: "Stateful AI agent with a 24/7 gateway, persistent memory, and channel integrations."
---

<Note>
**Stable pack.** OpenClaw is the flagship pack — a long-running agent with a gateway service, persistent state, cron jobs, multi-channel messaging (Telegram, Discord, Slack, WhatsApp, etc.), memory system, and sub-agent orchestration.
</Note>

## When to use

Pick OpenClaw when you want **an agent that remembers and runs continuously**:

- Ops assistant that keeps watch on your account
- Personal assistant accessible from messaging apps
- Coding agent with persistent memory across sessions
- Orchestrator for scheduled tasks + sub-agents

For one-shot "run this prompt and exit" workflows, a CLI pack ([claude-code](/agents/claude-code), [codex-cli](/agents/codex-cli), [kiro-cli](/agents/kiro-cli)) is simpler.

## What the pack installs

- Node.js + `openclaw` npm package via mise
- `bedrockify` dep (OpenAI-compatible Bedrock proxy on localhost)
- `openclaw-gateway` systemd user service on port 3001
- `~/.openclaw/` state directory on the 80 GB data volume
- `loki` and `openclaw` commands on `ec2-user`'s PATH

## Install — non-interactive

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Builder (recommended)">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile builder
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Account assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile account_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Personal assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack openclaw \
      --profile personal_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Pack parameters

Pack params are written to `/tmp/loki-pack-config.json` by `bootstrap.sh`. Simple-mode fills them automatically; advanced mode or manual pack invocation lets you override.

| Param | Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `region` | `--region` | `us-east-1` | AWS region for Bedrock |
| `model` | `--model` | `us.anthropic.claude-opus-4-6-v1` | Default Bedrock model ID |
| `port` | `--gw-port` | `3001` | Gateway port |
| `token` | *(auto)* | auto-generated | Gateway auth token |
| `model-mode` | `--model-mode` | `bedrock` | `bedrock`, `litellm`, or `api-key` |
| `litellm-url` | `--litellm-base-url` | — | LiteLLM base URL (model-mode=litellm) |
| `litellm-key` | `--litellm-api-key` | — | LiteLLM API key (model-mode=litellm) |
| `litellm-model` | `--litellm-model` | `claude-opus-4-6` | LiteLLM model ID |
| `provider-key` | `--provider-api-key` | — | Anthropic key (model-mode=api-key) |

## Resource requirements

| | Builder | Assistant profiles |
|---|---|---|
| Instance | `t4g.xlarge` | `t4g.medium` |
| Root volume | 40 GB | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 80 GB | 80 GB |
| Runtime | mise-managed Node.js | Same |

## First-run (post-install)

```bash
# SSM into the instance
aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>

# OpenClaw CLI is on PATH for ec2-user
openclaw --version
openclaw agent --agent main -m "Say hi"
```

The gateway starts as a systemd user unit:

```bash
systemctl --user status openclaw-gateway.service
```

## Connecting messaging channels

Post-install, configure channels from inside the gateway:

```bash
openclaw configure            # interactive setup
openclaw channels list        # show connected channels
```

Full channel docs live in OpenClaw's own docs at [docs.openclaw.ai](https://docs.openclaw.ai).

## Tear-down

Delete the CFN / Terraform stack. The data volume goes with it — back up `~/.openclaw/` first if you want to preserve state.
